gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) document is a scaffold with no human-authored content. The MSP defines the BoR as the organism's constitutional identity that provides scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, and human authority reservations. Without it, the Arbiter cannot enforce a scope gate, and Nomos cannot translate BoR articles into machine-actionable constraints. The DIO operates in default-permissive mode with no constitutional limits, leaving all actions ungoverned at the highest level.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "No Constitutional Foundation (Bill of Rights) – All Strategic Objectives Undefined",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all Articles undefined. Without a human-authored constitution, no scope of enterprise, core identity, prohibited activities, or human authority reservations exist. This means MSP cannot translate anything into machine-actionable objectives, leaving memory/context formation, dream-cycle health, and all DIO activities adrift without strategic grounding. Every aspect of the DIO’s operation lacks a legal/ethical perimeter.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights — Articles I through VI (all currently '[To be defined by human principal]'); MSP 'Purpose' section (cannot translate an empty BoR); MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' subsection (MSP lies subordinate to a void constitution).",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 10,
      "priority_score": 100
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "MSP Operational Rules Completely Absent – No Governance Over Dream Cycle or Memory Systems",
      "description": "The MSP 'Rules' section contains no rules, and the Genome References list is empty. The DIO operates in default-permissive mode, meaning there are no constraints, invariants, safety rules, or performance standards for the dream cycle, memory consolidation, or context freshness. The radiant/dreamer system runs without any machine-enforced quality gates, retention policies, or strategic alignment. The dream cycle is currently disabled, never run, and unmonitored — a direct consequence of having no operational mandate.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Section: Rules (states explicitly: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules.'); MSP Section: Genome References ('(none at seed)'); MSP Section: Purpose (states MSP translates BoR into machine-actionable objectives — impossible with empty BoR and no rules).",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact": 9,
      "priority_score": 81
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Dream Cycle Health Critical Failure – Scheduling Disabled, Health Never Run, No Watchdog",
      "description": "The dream cycle stats show 'scheduled: disabled', 'health: never_run', 'last_dream: null', and 'watchdog.grace_ms: 129600000' but no last check. This means the primary mechanism for context triage, memory consolidation, and systemic knowledge gardening is entirely offline. Without dream cycles, context blocks accumulate without pruning, memory entries drift without semantic merging, and the platform risks cognitive overload, reduced relevance, and eventual memory corruption. The inactive state directly violates any hypothetical strategic goal of maintaining a healthy, self-improving memory fabric.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Section: Rules (no rule requiring dream cycle operation); MSP Section: Genome References (no organ-specific rules for Radiant organ or dreamer skill); MSP Section: Amendment Protocol (no PEM has been generated to flag this gap because Nomos has no rules to evaluate dream cycle health).",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_score": 87
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Missing OKRs and Longer-Arc Goals for Memory/Context Formation",
      "description": "The MSP contains no mission objectives, priority programs, OKRs, or longer-arc goals, as called for in the Mission Frame header. Memory and context formation are fundamental to the DIO's learning and identity, yet there are no measurable targets (e.g., consolidation throughput, embedding coverage %, dream cycle cadence, context TTL compliance). The current stats show 72% embedding coverage, 31.7-day average memory age, and no freshness objectives — all of which should be governed by OKRs that don't exist.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Section: Purpose (translates BoR into 'precise, machine-actionable set of objectives' — no objectives exist); MSP Section: Rules (empty, so no priority programs or success criteria); BoR Article I (enterprise scope undefined, so mission boundary cannot be measured).",
      "criticality": 8,
      "urgency": 7,
      "impact": 9,
      "priority_score": 72
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Unregulated Memory/Context Proliferation Without Retention or Forgetfulness Policies",
      "description": "With no MSP rules governing context block TTLs, memory promotion criteria, consolidation quality thresholds, or sunset policies, the Radiant system accumulates data indiscriminately. Evidence: context blocks with overlapping content, multiple zombie entries (e.g., backup failures), and memory entries averaging 31.7 days without strategic pruning. This leads to noise, duplicate reasoning, and potential non-compliance with future data governance requirements. A BoR-defined scope of enterprise would naturally limit what can be retained, but that Article is empty.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Section: Rules (no retention/forgetfulness rules); MSP Section: Genome References (no Radiant-specific constraints); BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities — could forbid unbounded data retention, but undefined).",
      "criticality": 7,
      "urgency": 6,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_score": 56
    }
  ]
}

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "R-GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights scaffold without human-authored content",
      "description": "The entire Bill of Rights document is an empty scaffold. All six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) contain only ‘[To be defined by human principal]’. Consequently, the Mission Statement Protocol has no source principles to translate into constraints, and Nomos operates in a default-permissive mode with no defined boundary for enterprise actions. This is the fundamental relational gap: the human principal has not expressed the foundational commitments, limits, and identity that the DIO must respect.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights v1.0.0-seed, all articles; MSP ‘Purpose’ section stating the MSP translates the human-authored BoR; MSP ‘Relationship to the Bill of Rights’ section declaring the MSP strictly subordinate to the BoR.",
      "criticality": "maximum",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "total — the entire governance stack lacks its constitutional floor",
      "priority": "critical"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "R-GAP-002",
      "title": "Missing human‑principal communication channel for MSP amendments",
      "description": "The MSP Amendment Protocol requires that a draft amendment be placed into a ‘review’ state, that an HOM notification be emitted to the ‘human‑principal’ mailbox, and that the human principal respond with a POST /amend/review containing decision=approve. No mailbox address, contact method, or notification delivery mechanism is defined anywhere in the provided material. The Minder peer registry lists Leon as human, but contains no field for a mailbox endpoint or communication protocol. This gap prevents the MSP amendment lifecycle (and by extension any BoR authorship process) from ever reaching the human decision maker.",
      "grounding": "MSP ‘Amendment Protocol’ paragraph: exact quote of the notification and approval flow; Minder peer registry lacking any communication address.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "blocks all formal human-in-the-loop governance actions required by the MSP",
      "priority": "high"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "R-GAP-003",
      "title": "Absence of conversation history with the human principal",
      "description": "The Hippocampus slice ‘recent_conversations’ is empty, and no conversation summarisation data exists. While the Minder contains numerous deductive observations about Leon’s principles and preferences, there is no record of any direct dialogue in which Leon articulated those principles, gave feedback, made requests, or discussed the Bill of Rights. Person‑attention coverage thus relies entirely on inferred patterns rather than on captured authorial statements, and the system lacks the ability to refer back to what Leon actually said during interactions.",
      "grounding": "Hippocampus ‘recent_conversations’: [] ; implicit relational requirement in the MSP’s expectation that the human principal reviews and approves amendments and that the DIO must translate the ‘human-authored’ BoR.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "erodes trustworthiness and accuracy of person‑attention; risks misalignment between inferred observations and actual human intent",
      "priority": "high"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "R-GAP-004",
      "title": "No DIO‑to‑peer commitment tracking",
      "description": "The Minder contains observations about Leon, but no records of explicit commitments made by the DIO to Leon (or to any peer). The Bill of Rights, once authored, will contain prohibitions, reservations of authority, and other promises that the DIO must keep. The MSP itself may encode further obligations (e.g., that the Arbiter will verify BoR hash at every scope query). Currently there is no mechanism to capture, store, or later verify these DIO‑to‑human commitments, making it impossible to demonstrate relational accountability or to detect promise drift.",
      "grounding": "Future BoR Articles II (Prohibited Activities) and IV (Human Authority Reservation), which will create DIO commitments; MSP rules that create obligations to peers; the overall relational contract implied by the BoR/MSP governance model.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "long‑term trust maintenance and auditing becomes infeasible without an explicit commitment log",
      "priority": "medium"
    }
  ]
}
```
